serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of lemon coriander soup is approximately 250 g (one bowl), as typically served in North Indian restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Lemon Coriander Soup — estimated from ICMR-NIN component data (vegetable clear soup base, lemon juice, coriander leaves) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for individual ingredients
nutrient_source.organization: ICMR-NIN (Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single ICMR-NIN or USDA entry exists for lemon coriander soup as a composite dish. Values estimated from ICMR-NIN nutrient data for vegetable clear soup (thin broth base), fresh coriander leaves, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, and cornstarch thickener. The vegetarian variant uses vegetable stock; the non-vegetarian variant uses chicken stock, adding marginally more protein (~0.5 g/100 g). Values represent a midpoint estimate applicable to both variants. Energy and macros are consistent with published thin clear soup profiles in ICMR-NIN tables.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of vegetable-based clear soups and broth-based soups — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for vegetable soup and clear broth
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lemon coriander soup. GI estimated at 35 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for vegetable soup (GI ~30–40) and thin broth-based soups. The low carbohydrate content (~2.8 g/100 g), high water content, and acidic lemon component all support a low GI. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (35 × 2.8 × 2.5) / 100 ≈ 2.5 g, confirming very low GL. gi_category set to 'low' per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55).

hypertension.guidance: Lemon coriander soup contains approximately 280 mg sodium per 100 g (700 mg per 250 g serving), which represents a meaningful sodium load for hypertensive individuals; patients on sodium-restricted diets (<1500–2000 mg/day) should limit portion size or request a low-salt preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 35 and only ~2.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g, lemon coriander soup has a very low glycemic load (~2.5 per serving) and is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as a starter or light meal component, provided sodium intake is monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and beneficial phytonutrients from coriander and lemon
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lemon coriander soup is low in saturated fat (~0.05 g/100 g) and provides vitamin C and antioxidant phytonutrients from fresh coriander and lemon juice, making it a heart-friendly choice; however, the moderate sodium content warrants attention for patients with hypertension-related cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lemon Coriander Soup is a light, clear North Indian soup made by simmering a vegetable or chicken broth base with fresh coriander (cilantro) leaves, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, black pepper, and a small amount of cornstarch for body. It is a popular starter in Indian restaurants across Singapore and South Asia, served hot in a bowl. The soup has a bright, tangy flavour from the lemon juice, a fresh herbal aroma from the coriander, and a thin to slightly thickened consistency. It is available in both vegetarian (veg) and non-vegetarian (non-veg) variants; the non-veg version uses chicken stock, adding marginally more protein. Nutritionally, lemon coriander soup is very low in calories (~18 kcal/100 g), low in fat, and low in carbohydrates, making it suitable as a light starter for weight management and for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low glycemic load. It provides modest amounts of vitamin C, vitamin A, and folate from the coriander and lemon. The main nutritional concern is sodium, which can be elevated in restaurant preparations. Clinically, it is considered a low-risk food for cardiovascular disease and diabetes but should be consumed in moderation by hypertensive patients due to added salt.
common_user_queries: How many calories in lemon coriander soup?
Is lemon coriander soup good for diabetes?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for lemon coriander soup as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from ICMR-NIN component data for vegetable clear soup, coriander leaves, and lemon juice, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for individual ingredients. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for vegetable soup. Confidence is medium rather than low because the dish is compositionally simple, the ingredient nutrient profiles are well-documented, and the estimated values are internally consistent.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent a midpoint estimate for both veg and non-veg variants; the non-veg (chicken stock) variant may have slightly higher protein (~0.5 g/100 g more) and sodium. 2. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN or USDA SR Legacy for this soup category. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no added hydrogenated fats in this preparation). 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 for the vegetarian variant; the non-veg variant may contain trace amounts from chicken stock, but this is below detection threshold for the composite estimate — confirmed zero for the veg variant per ICMR-NIN plant-food data. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based broth with no animal fat or fortified ingredients; consistent with ICMR-NIN data for vegetable soups. 6. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 for the vegetarian variant; the non-veg variant may contain trace cholesterol from chicken stock, but the composite midpoint estimate is 0.0 — confirmed zero for veg variant. 7. GI set to 35 (analogous estimate, low category per GI ≤ 55 threshold rule). 8. Dietary flags reflect the veg variant as the conservative default; the non-veg variant is not vegan or vegetarian. 9. The food_group 'Mixed' is preserved from the input as the dish spans veg and non-veg preparations.
